name: Autodesk, Inc.

profile.description: Autodesk is the global leader in design and make software for architecture, engineering, construction, manufacturing, and media industries. The company serves over 4 million subscribers across 180 countries with cloud-based tools including AutoCAD, Revit, Fusion, and Maya, generating approximately $7 billion in annual revenue.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Autodesk is building industry-specific AI foundation models capable of understanding 2D/3D geometry, design data, and physical behavior. CEO Anagnost has positioned the company at the forefront of BIM, SaaS, generative design, and now generative AI. R&D spend of $393M in Q4 FY2025 alone demonstrates massive innovation investment, with AI-driven task, workflow, and system automations in active deployment.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Autodesk's mission of empowering designers and makers with transformative technology has driven industry standard-setting for decades. CEO Anagnost's vision of converging design and make in the cloud, enabled by AI, provides compelling strategic direction. The company's 97% subscription revenue model demonstrates deep customer alignment.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Autodesk delivered 18% revenue growth in Q3 FY2026 to approximately $7B full-year run rate, with RPO reaching $7.4B (20% growth). Raised FY2026 guidance of $7.03-$7.08B revenue with $9.80-$9.98 non-GAAP EPS. ROE of 39.7% demonstrates capital efficiency, though the 47.4x trailing P/E reflects premium expectations.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: Autodesk is positioning AI as a core growth driver rather than a threat, building generative AI tools that automate design tasks and create new product categories. The company's industry-specific AI foundation models and massive design data corpus provide a unique competitive advantage. AI is more likely to expand Autodesk's TAM by democratizing design than to commoditize it.

infinite_game.summary: Autodesk demonstrates infinite game thinking through its multi-decade evolution from desktop software to cloud-based SaaS, continuous investment in generative AI, and willingness to cannibalize legacy products for long-term platform value. The company consistently invests in capabilities that will define design and make for the next generation.

just_cause.analysis: Autodesk's mission of empowering people to design and make a better world has genuine aspirational quality. The company's tools enable sustainable building design, efficient manufacturing, and creative expression — outcomes with real social value.

trusting_teams.analysis: Autodesk employs approximately 14,000 people with a strong engineering culture. The company's successful cloud transition and AI investment reflect organizational alignment. Glassdoor ratings are generally positive, and the company's design-oriented culture attracts creative talent.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Autodesk competes effectively against Dassault Systemes, Siemens, PTC, and Bentley Systems. Its dominant market position in AEC (Architecture, Engineering, Construction) and 4 million subscriber base create a formidable competitive moat that rivals struggle to replicate.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The company has successfully navigated multiple technology transitions — from perpetual licensing to subscriptions, from desktop to cloud, and now from traditional tools to AI-powered generative design. The new transaction model adding ~$124M in Q3 shows continued business model innovation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Autodesk showed courage in its subscription transition, enduring short-term revenue pressure for long-term recurring revenue durability. The current investment in industry-specific AI foundation models demonstrates similar willingness to lead rather than follow technology transitions.

investment_thesis: Autodesk is the dominant design and make software platform with 4 million subscribers, 97% subscription revenue, and strong growth momentum at 18% revenue growth in Q3 FY2026. The company's investment in industry-specific AI foundation models positions it to define the next generation of design tools, while raised FY2026 guidance of $7.03-$7.08B revenue and $9.80-$9.98 EPS reflects accelerating execution at a forward P/E of 17.6x.
thesis_continued: The trailing P/E of 47.4x reflects premium expectations that leave limited margin for error. EPS growth of only 6.3% in the most recent period lags revenue growth, and cloud/AI workload scaling could pressure gross margins. Competition from Dassault, Siemens, and PTC in AI-enhanced design tools is intensifying, and macro uncertainty in construction and manufacturing sectors could slow subscription growth.
key_catalysts: Industry-specific AI foundation models for 2D/3D geometry and design automation could expand TAM and drive premium pricing
Remaining Performance Obligations of $7.4B growing 20% provide strong revenue visibility for coming quarters
New transaction model and AEC/manufacturing segment growth (20%+ and 23%+) accelerating above-seasonal performance
key_risks: Premium trailing P/E of 47.4x creates vulnerability to any growth deceleration or macro slowdown in construction/manufacturing
Cloud and AI workload scaling could pressure gross margins even as they are accretive to gross profit dollars
Intensifying AI competition from Dassault Systemes, Siemens, and PTC developing rival generative design capabilities
